Entity_Description: Crofton is a seaside village on the east coast of Vancouver Island, British Columbia, within the Municipality of North Cowichan in the Cowichan Valley. Founded in 1902 by Henry Croft as a smelter town for copper ore from the Mount Sicker mines, it recorded a population of about 2,500 in the 2021 Census. Its economy centres on the Crofton pulp and paper mill on Osborne Bay, and a BC Ferries terminal links the village to Vesuvius Bay on Salt Spring Island.

Long_Description:
Crofton began in 1902 when Henry Croft laid out a townsite to smelt copper ore hauled down from the Mount Sicker mines, and that industrial DNA still defines the village today. The smelter is long gone, but the Crofton pulp and paper mill on Osborne Bay carried the working-waterfront tradition forward and remains one of the largest employers anchoring the local economy. That single fact tells a {BUSINESS_TYPE} a great deal about the {PROPERTY_TYPE} here and the kind of {SERVICE} it demands.

The village itself is compact and walkable, its streets stepping uphill from the Crofton Seawalk and the BC Ferries terminal that shuttles residents across Stuart Channel to Vesuvius Bay on Salt Spring Island. The housing tells the community's story in layers: modest mill-town cottages from the smelter and pulp-mill eras, 1970s-to-90s ranchers, and newer ocean-view homes and retirement builds catching the light off the Gulf Islands. Each vintage carries its own {SERVICE} needs, and {COMPANY_NAME} treats a heritage cottage near the water differently than a modern rancher on the upper streets.

The setting on Osborne Bay is a defining condition, not just scenery. Salt air, damp Pacific winters, and rainfall heavier than the drier inland pockets of the island put constant pressure on exterior materials, fasteners, and finishes. A {BUSINESS_TYPE} who doesn't account for that marine exposure will see work fail early; our crews plan for it from the start, which is exactly what a coastal village like Crofton requires from its {SERVICE}.

Crofton also sits at a convenient hub within the Cowichan Valley. Chemainus is only about seven kilometres north, North Cowichan surrounds the community, and Duncan is roughly eleven kilometres away, so a single trip can cover several neighbouring jobs. {COMPANY_NAME} serves the entire area, and locals appreciate that we know it firsthand rather than treating Crofton as an afterthought on a regional map.

Whether your property overlooks the ferry dock, sits along the seawalk, or climbs the slope above the bay, {COMPANY_NAME} brings {SERVICE} built for how Crofton actually lives and weathers. Reach us at {PHONE} to get started.
Local_Facts:
- Population: 2,500 (2021 Census)
- Located in the Municipality of North Cowichan, Cowichan Valley, on the east coast of Vancouver Island
- Founded in 1902 by Henry Croft as a copper smelter town serving the Mount Sicker mines
- Home to the Crofton pulp and paper mill on Osborne Bay, a major regional employer
- A BC Ferries terminal connects Crofton to Vesuvius Bay on Salt Spring Island
- Set on Osborne Bay overlooking Stuart Channel and the Gulf Islands, with a mild, wet coastal climate
- Neighbouring communities include Chemainus (about 7 km) and Duncan (about 11 km)
